# Claude Code prompt — [5A.2] Quote PDF — restore brand styling, fix page-break + document-control block

*Paste everything below the line into Claude Code. Generated by Cowork, 2026-05-26.*

---

You are working on `GH-Mantova/ProjectOperations`. Read `CLAUDE.md` and follow
it. This is a fix-forward PR for roadmap §5A.2 (Quote PDF). PR #223 is merged —
it fixed the doubled / overlapping repeating header by moving the header to
Puppeteer's `headerTemplate`. That worked. But it lost the IS brand styling,
dropped a document-control element, and visual review of live output surfaced a
layout issue plus two smaller items. This PR cleans all of it up.

**Hard constraint — do not regress #223.** The repeating header MUST stay on
Puppeteer's `headerTemplate` mechanism. Do NOT reintroduce an in-page or
`position: fixed` header element — that was the doubling / overlap bug #223
eliminated.

## Phase 0 — pre-work

- `git fetch origin`; branch off `main`: `fix/quote-pdf-brand-and-layout`.
- Conflict-check the files you will touch (the HTML quote builder, the PDF
  renderer call sites, `docs/samples/*.pdf`, `progress.md`, `roadmap.md`).
- Inspect the quote builder's history before PR #223 (`git show` on
  `quote-html.builder.ts`) to see the original `headerBand()` /
  `fixedHeaderBand()` brand styling — you will reproduce that look in the
  header / footer templates.

## Task 1 — restore IS teal brand styling to the repeating header AND footer

- Style the `headerTemplate` as the IS teal brand band: teal background, white
  text, the IS logo (already embedded as base64 in #223), "INITIAL SERVICES",
  the licence line, and "Quote No. &lt;ref&gt;". Match the original pre-#223
  brand design.
- Apply matching teal brand styling to the **footer** via `footerTemplate` —
  the footer is currently plain text (address line + "Page X of Y"). Make it a
  teal brand band consistent with the header; keep the address line and page
  numbering.
- Puppeteer header / footer template quirks to handle: background colours
  require `-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact` (and `print-color-adjust:
  exact`); template default font-size is tiny — set explicit sizes; size the
  page `margin.top` / `margin.bottom` so both bands fit fully without clipping
  and without overlapping body content.

## Task 2 — restore the document-control block

- Re-add the "Electronic document / Uncontrolled when printed / Printed on:
  &lt;date&gt;" block. It appeared on every page before #223; #223 dropped it.
  Put it in the `headerTemplate` (Puppeteer's headerTemplate has a built-in
  `.date` span for the printed date). It must render on every page.

## Task 3 — fix the page-break / content-flow issue

- Symptom: when the Scope of Works section is short, page 2 ends up ~80% empty
  and the following section starts fresh on page 3 — a large white gap. Cause:
  a hard page break isolates the Preliminary Works / Referenced Drawings /
  Scope of Works section onto its own page.
- Fix: remove that unconditional page break so sections flow and fill the
  page; break only when a page is genuinely full.
- The Scope of Works table is **variable length** — a few rows on some jobs,
  many on others. The layout must handle both: a short table must not leave a
  blank page; a long table must break across pages cleanly (the column-header
  row repeats if it spans a page; no orphaned single rows).

## Task 4 — investigate the missing Scope of Works table

- A freshly generated quote (ref `IS-T020-R3`) renders with **no Scope of
  Works table at all**. The builder *can* render one —
  `docs/samples/sample-quote-tender-level.pdf` from #223 shows a Scope of
  Works table — so this is specific to that quote, not a broken builder.
- Investigate: does the `IS-T020-R3` quote / its tender have Scope of Works
  line items in the data? Is the overlay's `showScopeTable` flag off for it?
- If it is a builder bug (scope items exist and should display but do not
  render) — fix it. If it is a data gap (no scope items) or an overlay flag
  set off — do NOT invent data or override the flag; report the finding in the
  PR body so the owner can decide.

## Task 5 — stray ")" in Cost Summary labels

- The generated `IS-T020-R3` quote shows each Cost Summary scope label ending
  with a stray ")": e.g. "Civil works)", "Asbestos removal (Class A + B))".
  It is consistent across every row.
- Check whether the builder is appending the ")". If so, fix it. If the ")" is
  in the source data, do not change data — report it in the PR body.

## Regenerate + verify

- Regenerate both `docs/samples/` PDFs (the IS-T020 tender-level quote, which
  has scope items, and the ClientQuote-with-overlay sample).
- Programmatic checks: each renders without error; the header block and the
  footer block each appear exactly once per page on every page; the
  document-control text is present on every page; the Scope of Works table
  renders for the demo tender.
- Final visual confirmation (teal bands actually rendering in colour, no
  overlap, no blank-page gap) is done by Cowork — see the PR step below.
- Run the full §6 pre-PR CI checklist; the `api` job must be green.
